commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: Behold mercy and judgment—mercy on the elect, who have obtained the righteousness of God, but judgment upon the others who have been blinded. And yet the former have believed because they willed it, while the latter have not believed because they have not willed it. Hence mercy and judgment were executed in their own wills.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Do these words need any interpretation? Are they not plain to everyone? Homilies on Romans
